exemplar.title:
How, and to what effect, do Tennesee Williams and Federico Garcia
Lorca convey ideas about the oppression of women in A Streetcar Named Desire and The House of Bernarda Alba, respectively?

detailedExemplarData.title:
How, and to what effect, do Tennesee Williams and Federico Garcia
Lorca convey ideas about the oppression of women in A Streetcar Named Desire and The House of Bernarda Alba, respectively?

subject.title: English Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Literature focuses exclusively on the study of literature, developing students' appreciation and critical analysis of literary works from various cultures, periods, and genres. Students examine poetry, prose, and drama through close reading, comparative analysis, and exploration of literary techniques and themes. The course cultivates sophisticated interpretive skills and deep understanding of how literature reflects and shapes human experience across different cultural contexts.

strengths: Topic is announced clearly in title and sustained throughout with clear sectional headings
Research question is sharply focused and repeatedly connected to analysis
Comparative framework remains visible in major sections, supporting a coherent line of inquiry
weaknesses: Introduction contains an overly lengthy synopsis that dilutes focus
Methodological approach is only implied rather than explicitly justified
Occasional digressions into broader historical background weaken tightness of focus
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay communicates its topic and purpose accurately and maintains focus on the research question throughout, meriting a mark in the 5â6 band. However, minor lapsesâparticularly a long introductory synopsis, implicit rather than explicit methodology, and a few contextual digressionsâprevent a perfect score. A score of 5 reflects a consistently clear and effective focus with scope for minor tightening.

strengths: Demonstrates excellent command of both dramas with detailed textual references
Terminology (e.g., Naturalism, anagnorisis, auditory stagecraft) is used accurately and fluently
Secondary criticism is woven in to illuminate rather than merely decorate analysis
weaknesses: Occasional factual slip in conflated publication/performance dates
Limited engagement with the most recent scholarship on either dramatist
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows coherent and confident knowledge of both primary texts and relevant critical sources, integrating specialized terminology with precision. Occasional factual inaccuracies and somewhat limited use of newer scholarship prevent the top mark. A score of 5 acknowledges strong understanding and effective source use, with room to expand and update evidence.

strengths: Research is substantial, engaging primary and secondary sources critically and mostly relevant to the question
Analysis offers nuanced readings of imagery and stagecraft with well-supported conclusions
Argument builds comparative evaluation with integration of theoretical perspectives (e.g., Hirjiâs prudential good)
weaknesses: Some passages lapse into narrative summary rather than deep analysis
Reliance on a single theoretical lens (Frye) limits the breadth of perspectives
Evaluation of certain cultural assumptions (e.g., Spanish context) lacks sufficient evidentiary support
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall the essay demonstrates strong critical thinking, with good research relevance and depth of analysis, situating evidence in service of a focused argument. Although evaluation is generally sound, occasional over-reliance on one theory and underdeveloped cultural contextualization keep it in the upper 7â9 band. A score of 9 reflects very good critical engagement with room for further breadth and self-reflection.

strengths: All required structural elements are present and logically ordered (title page, contents, introduction, body, conclusion, bibliography)
Sectional headings and internal paragraphing guide the reader clearly
Layout adheres to IB formatting requirements overall
weaknesses: Minor formatting issues (inconsistent superscripts, overly detailed footnotes) interrupt polish
Table of contents lacks page numbers for direct navigation
A few dense paragraphs could be broken up to improve readability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation fully meets IB structural and formatting requirements, with a clear and coherent organization deserving the top of the 3â4 band. Although there are small typographical and navigational lapses, these do not impede overall readability. A mark of 4 recognises the strength of structure and layout with only minor areas for refinement.

strandSelections.reasoning: The essay cites primary texts meticulously and draws upon a reasonable spread of secondary sources (Frye, Hirji, Britannica, historical articles).  Selection appears purposeful, yet the range is moderate rather than extensive and a few websites of doubtful academic weight (e.g., âMr Hoyeâs IB websiteâ) dilute scholarly rigour.  Methodological choices are described implicitly rather than explicitly justified, giving a generally competent but not fully âcomplete and effectiveâ methodology.  Upper 3-4 band, 4 marks, is appropriate.

Formal elements (title, word count, in-text citations, bibliography) are present, and numbering appears in the extract.  Minor formatting lapses â visible markup around footnotes, inconsistent superscript notation, occasional spacing errors â reduce overall polish.  Requirements are met but not flawlessly executed, so 3 marks within the 3-4 band are appropriate.

Research is substantial and, for the most part, directly relevant to the question.  Primary evidence is plentiful and secondary voices are engaged critically rather than reproduced.  Some contextual sources are tangential or only loosely connected, so complete consistency is lacking; nevertheless, the overall research quality is clearly âgoodâ and mostly pertinent, sitting at the lower end of the 7-9 band with 7 marks.

The studentâs topic â a comparative exploration of the oppression of women in two canonical dramas â is announced in the title, reiterated in the introduction and sustained throughout the essay.  The purpose (to examine how each dramatist represents Fryeâs âdouble-bindâ) remains visible in every major section, and clear sectional headings ensure the reader can follow the intended line of inquiry.  Minor lapses â e.g., a somewhat lengthy introductory synopsis and occasional digressions into historical background â slightly blur the tightness of focus, so the strand sits solidly but not perfectly in the 5-6 band; 5 marks are the best fit.

The discussion builds a coherent comparative argument, culminating in a thoughtful evaluation of each authorâs differing treatment of autonomy.  Counter-examples (e.g., Blancheâs purity vs Adelaâs strength) are acknowledged.  Evaluation is present but not consistently critical of the essayâs own assumptions, and some generalisations (about Spanish multiculturalism) are asserted rather than evidenced.  The top of the 4-6 band, 6 marks, best reflects this balanced but not fully sophisticated evaluation.

Analytical passages closely unpack language and stagecraft (âwhite vs brownâ, âfiery fingersâ, sound patterning), link evidence to argument and return to the research question.  Conclusions are usually well grounded.  A few sections lapse into narrative summary or broad contextual comment, slightly weakening sustained depth, so âexcellentâ is only partially achieved.  7 marks at the lower edge of the 7-9 band are fitting.

All required structural elements (title page, contents, introduction, body sections, conclusion, bibliography) are present and logically ordered.  Sectional headings guide the reader effectively; internal paragraphing is coherent.  The structure fully meets IB requirements, warranting the top of the 3-4 band with 4 marks.

The student demonstrates excellent command of both plays: detailed scene/act references, accurate synopsis, and nuanced awareness of Naturalist conventions.  Secondary criticism is woven in to illuminate points rather than decorate them.  Very occasional factual slips (e.g., conflated publication/performance dates) and limited engagement with more recent scholarship prevent the highest possible mark, but knowledge remains coherent and confidently applied, justifying 5 marks in the top band.

Subject-specific terminology â Naturalism, stage directions, anagnorisis, auditory stagecraft, imagery, modal verbs â is used accurately and fluently.  Literary-critical concepts (double-bind, prudential good) are integrated naturally into analysis.  A handful of minor typographical inconsistencies do not impede meaning.  Consistency and accuracy justify 5 marks within the 5-6 band.

The research question (How, and to what effect, do Williams and Lorca convey ideas about the oppression of womenâ¦ ?) is explicitly stated, sharply delimited to two works and to a specific conceptual lens.  The body paragraphs repeatedly return to âhowâ (stagecraft, imagery, structure) and âeffectâ (audience sympathy, social critique), demonstrating strong alignment between question and discussion.  Occasional moments drift into contextual history that is not directly tied back to the wording of the question, preventing a perfect score; 5 marks are merited.

annotations.comment: The title page effectively presents the investigation topic and clearly states the research question, demonstrating strong adherence to IB formatting requirements.
Including the word count on the title page confirms the essay meets IB requirements and stays within the 4000-word limit.
The absence of a visible page number on the title page interrupts consistency; adding a footer page number here would improve formatting flow.
The table of contents lists all essay sections logically, guiding the reader through the argument structure effectively.
The table of contents lacks page numbers for each section, reducing navigational clarity; include precise page references.
The introduction provides a detailed synopsis, but its length diminishes focus on the research question; consider condensing background material.
Methodological approach is only implied through source citations; include a brief explicit methodology section explaining selection criteria and research process.
The OCR misrendering âTrilog y^4â disrupts clarity; correct to âTrilogyâ to maintain professional presentation.
The definition and quotation of Fryeâs âdouble-bindâ are well integrated, showing excellent understanding of key theoretical concepts.
The thesis is restated twice in close succession on this page; consolidate these statements to present a tighter focus.
The literature review on Naturalist drama is well executed with accurate use of secondary sources like Zola, demonstrating strong topic knowledge.
Analysis relies heavily on Fryeâs theory; consider comparing additional critical perspectives to enrich your argument.
Footnote explaining the Varsouviana Polka is overly detailed and detracts from flow; shorten or relocate to bibliography.
The shift to Lorcaâs play is clearly signposted, maintaining focus on your comparative framework.
Your integration of Hirjiâs âprudential goodâ concept enriches the comparative evaluation and demonstrates critical thinking.
No reflection on research challenges or decision-making appears; include an engagement/reflection section to meet IB criteria.
Consider adding a brief reflection on your planning and setbacks to demonstrate personal engagement with the research process.
Bibliography entries display inconsistent formatting (italic titles, URL breaks); apply a uniform citation style throughout.
Relying on encyclopedia entries and websites weakens scholarly rigour; integrate more peer-reviewed or recent journal articles to strengthen methodology.
Footnote 12 combines multiple source details in one entry, affecting clarity; split into separate citations for consistency.
Your analysis connects setting and atmosphere to the theme of claustrophobia powerfully, reflecting strong depth of interpretation.
This paragraph tends toward narrative summary; strengthen critical analysis by explicitly linking observations back to your research question.
The detailed reading of colour and insect imagery in Blancheâs entrance exhibits nuanced literary analysis and strong textual engagement.
Citation numbering misprint (â338â instead of â38â) disrupts reference accuracy; review all superscripts for consistency.
Link your discussion of auditory stagecraft back to the double-bind concept explicitly to reinforce coherence of analysis.
The use of Varsouviana music imagery convincingly conveys Blancheâs mental deterioration and societal pressure.
Consider adding transitional phrasing (e.g., âTurning to Lorcaâs depictionâ¦â) to highlight the comparative move more explicitly.
Assertions about Spainâs religious roots are broad and unsupported here; cite specific historical evidence to substantiate this point.
The foil analysis of Martirio is introduced but not fully developed; expand on how her experiences contrast with Adelaâs.
Briefly recapping the act or scene context before analysis could help readers unfamiliar with Lorcaâs structure.
Your discussion of anagnorisis effectively synthesizes Greek tragic theory with Fryeâs double-bind, showcasing high-level integration.
Footnote 61 is overly lengthy and narrative; streamline to the essential citation to maintain academic tone.
The paragraph starting âAfter this realisationâ¦â is very long and dense; break it into two for improved readability.
Consider adding more critical reflection on the influence of Spanish cultural context here to deepen evaluative insight.
The overall evaluation deftly synthesizes comparative findings and clearly ties back to the research question.
Alphabetize bibliography entries by author surname to enhance organization and adhere to academic conventions.
